[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016-04-10
[TITLE]: David Rockefeller hosts some of the world’s wealthiest at MOMA | New York Post
[METADATA/INTRO]: If you wanted to meet some of the world’s richest men, then lunch on the third floor of The Museum of Modern Art on Thursday was the place to find them. David Rockefeller, 100 years old, hosted his friends in...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: If you wanted to meet some of the world’s richest men, then lunch on the third floor of The Museum of Modern Art on Thursday was the place to find them. 
 David Rockefeller, 100 years old, hosted his friends in high finance for a classy lunch of champagne, lobster salad, roast chicken and fava beans. His mother, Abby Aldrich Rockefeller, helped found the museum. 
 Guests included KKR’s Henry Kravis and wife Marie-Josee, Apollo chief Leon Black and real estate mogul and museum Chairman Jerry Speyer. Other attendees included Ron Lauder and Gustavo Cisneros. 
 The lunch was in honor of BlackRock boss Larry Fink, who proclaimed, “I have definitely become a New Yorker,” despite being born in Van Nuys, Calif. We’re glad Fink is proud of our New York values, even if political candidates aren’t.
